title: Preserved Guavas
excerpt: We grew up on preserved guavas and custard. It was a real treat for us. It was our Sunday pudding. Making it is just as easy. And it's guava season now. The best guavas are the ones that aren't too ripe. The syrup formula is as follows: …
html:
We grew up on preserved guavas and custard. It was a real treat for us. It was our Sunday pudding. Making it is just as easy. And it's guava season now.

The best guavas are the ones that aren't too ripe. The syrup formula is as follows: 250 ml sugar for every 500 ml water.

Ingredients

  • 4 kg peeled guavas
  • 2 L water
  • 4 cups sugar
  • 4 cinnamon sticks

Method

  1. sterilise your jars by placing them in a pot of water and quickly “boiling” them (really they just need to steam) for 5 minutes. Take out and let dry on a rack
  2. boil the water, sugar and cinnamon sticks for 10 minutes
  3. meanwhile peel your guavas and cut them in half
  4. cook the guavas in the syrup for 5 minutes
  5. put the guavas in the bottles and fill to the top with the syrup – I just pop a cinnamon stick into each jar
  6. seal tightly and store
